ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu tumi o tumusi, ba pa yii we o beeri Isawu saani, a yi Seya n ba Idom paaligu.
translated: The chief and Yakubu sent a message, and they said they would meet at Isawu's place, in the house of Seya in Idom's village.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
text: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, “Cheli ti maali nisaaladima, ba ŋmaani ti, ka mari daama, a yi koom puu koroto niŋi, niinsi, a ni tiŋgbaŋŋu buŋkobiri zaa niŋi.”
translated: The Creator said, "Let the rivers be filled with fish, and let there be trees, and let there be animals, and let the forests and the grasslands be filled with all kinds of game animals."

ref: God knew that everyone was terribly cruel and violent.
text: Niŋi n na, Naawunni nyaasi durinye niriba maa, ka ba zaa niŋini yabeniŋiniba.
translated: The chief and the Naawunni's grass has overgrown the people's town, and they are all crying out in pain.

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Ibrahim ni Saara zaa daa n kurigini, haali, Saara daa n bi lee n pegira.
translated: Ibrahim and Saara went to the farm, meanwhile, Saara went and gave birth to a child.

ref: Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah to meet him in the pasture where he kept his sheep,

ref: Her brother Laban heard her tell what the servant had said, and he saw the ring and the bracelets she was wearing. So Laban ran out to Abraham's servant, who was standing by his camels at the well.
text: Rabeeka sunzodaba Leban n si n nye o ŋoya kunde la ni bensi la n ba o sunzopoga nuusi, ka wumi seema Rabeeka n yeli bunso daba maa n yeli u, o yi yoom gaa daba maa saani, a wa ti nye o n za n begi o nyogimasi, a yi kuliga maa noori.
translated: Rabeeka got up early in the morning and went to the farm, but when she got to the farm, she realized she had forgotten the key to the shed, so she went back home to look for it, when she got home, she told her husband that she had forgotten the key, her husband told her to go back to the farm and he would bring the key, she went back to the farm and waited, but her husband didn't come.

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.
text: Ama, a kpali naaba maa n naa Abram poga Sarai gaari o laanta la zu, Duuma ti toorabeeri gbaa o ni o laanta dima zaa.
translated: Sarah, Abram's wife, took Hagar the Egyptian and her son and sent them away, and they wandered in the wilderness of Beersheba.

ref: Jacob replied, “I am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.”
text: Yakubu yeli o ba, “Mani Isawu, e bikpeema n na. M niŋi seema e ni yeli ma ni n niŋi la, a zu, nyisigi n zi, a di bindira maa, ka nafa ma.”
translated: Yakubu said to her, "Mother Isawu, I am very hungry. I have done what you told me to do, but I am tired, my body aches, I am eating this food, and it is benefiting me."

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: “Ma boosi u, ‘Ŋani bii n niŋi i?’ O yeli, ‘M ba n niŋi Betuwel, Naho ni Milika biya.’ Niŋi n na, ma naa kunde niŋi o ŋoya, ka naa bensi niŋi o nusogsi.
translated: They asked him, "What is your name?" He said, "My name is Betuwel, son of Nahor and Milcah." After that, they gave him a sheep's leg, and also gave him a bottle of milk.

ref: This includes the birds and the animals that came out of the boat.
text: a ni binvuusira zaa, niinsi, buŋkobiri a ni bunso zaa n paasi ya niŋi yi ŋariŋu la puwa.
